title: Prices continue in the gap direction on gap days; gaps do not systematically fill (Plastun et al.)
claim: On days with an opening price gap, prices tend to continue moving in the direction of the gap during that day (intraday momentum after gaps); the momentum effect is temporary (same-day, not multi-day), there is no seasonality in gap occurrence, and the popular belief that gaps get filled is not supported.

source.label: Plastun, Sibande, Gupta & Wohar (2020), 'Price gap anomaly in the US stock market: The whole story', North American Journal of Economics and Finance 52
source.quote: during a gap day prices tend to change in the direction of the gap

rule_formal: Gap day: Open_t != Close_{t-1} beyond a threshold; test H0: distribution of (Close_t - Open_t) sign/magnitude independent of sign(Open_t - Close_{t-1}); finding: E[Close_t - Open_t | gap up] > 0 and symmetric for gap down (continuation, not fill).

numbers.caveats: Daily-frequency study on equity indices; abstract fetch gives direction of findings but no return magnitudes; does not state control for weekend/holiday gaps — the exact confound that killed the Trellis gap variable is not addressed in the abstract

mechanism: Gaps embody overnight information whose incorporation is incomplete at the open (orders placed before open only partially absorb the news); remaining underreaction resolves as same-direction intraday drift — momentum is temporary because it is a within-day incorporation process
relevance: Q3: supports SIGNED gap direction as an intraday directional conditioner (breakouts aligned with gap direction should follow through more) — distinct from the falsified unsigned |gap|/prev_range magnitude filter; also directly falsifies the gap-fill (fade) alternative, so the fade direction need not be tested

test_design.lookahead_risk: Gap sign known at day open (01:00 EET or first tick), hours before the 08:00-20:00 entry window — no lookahead

overlap_risk: High measurement overlap with dead variable |overnight gap|/prev_range (p=0.035 raw, dies controlling days-since-prev/weekend) — BUT dead variable was unsigned day filter, this is signed direction-alignment; any test must inherit the weekend/days-since-prev control that killed the unsigned version
